response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented polyglot engineer with genuine low-latency systems exposure, but he misses the role's core minimum qualification: his professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, far short of the required 8 years. His distributed-systems work (about two years at Sociomantic, in DLang) and cloud experience (product-level AWS/Kubernetes) fall short of the 5 years of large-scale infrastructure depth expected, and he has no formal technical-leadership role owning cross-team strategy. His profile fits a senior full-stack or product engineering role far better than a C++ cloud compute platform lead.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored open-source testing tools
Proven mentor and knowledge-sharer through workshops, courses, and community talks
concerns: Roughly 7 months of professional C++ versus the required 8 years; C++ is absent from his current skills list
No formal technical-lead or management experience driving strategy, priorities, and cross-team alignment
Large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture depth is limited (~2 years, in DLang), well under the 5-year requirement

key_factors.factor: Limited large-scale infrastructure and technical-leadership depth
